Wangneng Optoelectronics (Wujiang) Co., Ltd. presents a profound anomaly indicative of a severely distressed or structurally repurposed Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) mega-project. Registered as a wholly-owned Hong Kong/Macao/Taiwan entity with a staggering fully paid-in capital of 120 million USD, it was clearly designed as a massive solar cell manufacturing base. However, its 2024 data reveals only 6 insured employees. A team of 6 cannot operate a 120M USD solar wafer/cell production line. This catastrophic ratio confirms that the physical manufacturing operations in the Wujiang Economic Development Zone have been entirely suspended, liquidated, or relocated. The entity now exists purely as a highly capitalized 'Zombie Shell'—likely utilized by its Taiwanese parent company (Neo Solar Power, inferred from the @nsp.com domain) for land holding, asset liquidation, or cross-border financial routing rather than active manufacturing.
